question: In question number 21 above, choose the best option from letters A - D that best completes the gap.
section:

Believe it or not, change is to human existence what the blood is to the human body. We live in era of amazing .... 16.... [A. well- defined B. fast-paced C. favourable D. social] change spawned by advancing technology and industrialization. However, man’s ...17.... [A. knowledge of B. attitude to C. commitment to D. opinion of] promoting and defending change in a deliberate effort to establish...18... [A. customs B. companies C. trade- zones D. variations] that stimulate advancement for man’s concern is proving unfavourable to the climate with threatening ...19... [A. repercussions B. clouds C. pressure D. implication].

Human-induced climate change has awakened widespread concern across the globe. As a matter of fact, climate change is now 20 [A. an acceptable B. a foremost C. the only D. the last] global issue. It is a major test of Africa’s ....21.... [A. popularity B. energy C. ingenuity D. incapability] ! the fourth Assessment Report {AR4} of the intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) confirmed that human actions are changing the earth’s climate and creating maker disturbances in human ....22...[A. geography B. societies C. systems D life] and ecosystems. The IPCC reports that the world has warned by an average of 0.76oC since pre-industrial times. The rising global ...23.... [A. command B. demand C. warming D. supply] for energy and the adverse changes in the climate put the earth and its inhabitants in a catch -22 situation. Again, if the effects of climate change on each were commensurate with the level of greenhouse ...24 .... [A. structure B. paints C. emulsion D. emissions] in spews out, perhaps Africa would have been spared and would probably be just an amused spectator. But it is, this is not the case. Here again, we see well-meaning global citizens appealing for the rest of the world to take responsibility for the problems of Africa, a strategy that cannot, thus far, termed ....25.... [A. notable B. liable C. credible D. flexible]
solution: Ingenuity; the quality of being clever, original, and inventive.

question: When his car tyre ......... on the way, he did not know what to do.
section: In each of the questions 64 to 83, choose the option that best completes the gap(s).
solution:
When his car tyre burst on the way, he did not know what to do.

Although bursted is not a word in English, some writers erroneously use it as a past tense or past participle for burst. As mentioned above, burst is itself the past tense form of this verb, making bursted unnecessary and incorrect.

question: The warring communities were <u>coerced</u> into negotiating a settlement?
section: In each of the questions 49 to 63 choose the word that is opposite in meaning to the underlined word.
solution:
coerced; obtain (something) from someone by using force or threats.

persuaded; provide a sound reason for (someone) to do something.

question: Okon's company took a hit last year?

option.b: His company made a huge success last year
option.c: His company was badly damaged last year
option.d: His company was established last year

section: LEXIS, STRUCTURE AND ORAL FORMS In each of the questions 26 to 33, select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.
solution: To suffer heavy losses or be badly affected (by something).

question: Ife asked me ....... ?

option.b: what is it by my time

section: In each of the questions 64 to 83, choose the option that best completes the gap(s).
solution: Ife asked me what time it was.

question: The mistake brought the show to an <u>ignominious</u> end?
section: In each of the questions 34 to 48, Choose the option nearest in meaning to the underlined word(s).
solution: Ignominious; deserving or causing public disgrace or shame.

question: She stopped her education as her uncle left her in the lurch?

option.c: Her uncle refused to help her

section: LEXIS, STRUCTURE AND ORAL FORMS In each of the questions 26 to 33, select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.
solution: To abandon somebody; especially, to abandon somebody and leave him or her in a difficult situation.

question: Jummai is <u>cruel</u> to her husband?
section: In each of the questions 49 to 63 choose the word that is opposite in meaning to the underlined word.
solution:
Cruel; wilfully causing pain or suffering to others, or feeling no concern about it.

passionate; having, showing, or caused by strong feelings or beliefs.

question: Laraba saw a <u>forlorn</u> little figure sitting outside the class?
section: In each of the questions 34 to 48, Choose the option nearest in meaning to the underlined word(s).
solution: forlorn; pitifully sad and abandoned or lonely.

question: Agoshito is a <u>callow</u> youth', said the teacher?
section: In each of the questions 49 to 63 choose the word that is opposite in meaning to the underlined word.
solution: Callow; (of a young person) inexperienced and immature.

question: <u>Kn</u>ot?
section: In each of the questions 84 to 89 choose the option that has the same vowel sound as the one represented by the underlined letter(s).

question: Getting a well paid job nowadays is an ...... task?
section: In each of the questions 64 to 83, choose the option that best completes the gap(s).
solution:
Getting a well paid job nowadays is an uphill task?

When something is an uphill task or an uphill struggle, it's a difficult thing to do.
